headline: Four Orphaned Bobkittens Get Second Chance at Life
meta_description: Four baby bobcats orphaned across California found hope at a wildlife sanctuary, learning survival skills before their October release into the wild.
summary_short: Four orphaned bobkittens rescued from different California locations are thriving at a wildlife sanctuary, preparing for life in the wild this October.
body:
Within days of each other, four baby bobcats arrived at the Saratoga Wildlife Care Center—each with a heartbreaking backstory. Two were found in Kern after their mother died in a fall. Another came from Carmel, and a fourth from Concord. Their mothers had either perished or abandoned them, leaving their survival in doubt.

The Peninsula Humane Society & SPCA coordinated the rescues, bringing all four under one roof where they received treatment for parasites and a natural meat diet in specialized recovery facilities. Now, months later, the kittens are thriving.

"They have a large enclosure where they're conditioning, they're building muscle, they're learning how to pounce on things," said a caregiver overseeing their development. "They're very curious."

The team is careful not to let care become companionship. The kittens remain wary of humans—exactly as they should be—and are on track for introduction into the wild as semi-adults in October. This balance between nurturing and independence will determine whether these resilient animals can survive on their own.

For those who encounter mewling bobkittens in the wild, experts advise caution: contact your nearest wildlife rescue rather than attempting rescue yourself. A trained professional can determine whether intervention is truly needed.
